content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 958 l-i-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fall (916) 322-0886 
Dan Miller, Filing Officer 
Office of Registrar of Voters 
PO Box E 
San Rafael, CA 94913 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-11-018 
Dear Mr. Miller: 
February 3, 2011 
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the public inspection 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the .. Act").1 Your question concerns Marin County's 
obligations regarding the Internet posting of statements of economic interests. 
QUESTION 
May a local government agency post on the agency's website a copy of complete or 
redacted Form 700 filed by elected officials and designated employees? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The Act does not regulate the posting of public officials' Form 700 on a local 
agency's website. Therefore, an agency may post a copy of the entire or redacted form on the 
website. 
FACTS 
You are the filing officer for the County of Marin. The county currently provides public 
access to copies of Form 700 filed by elected officials and designated employees in the form of 
; 
intrnnet, email and paper copies. You would like to post the complete or a redacted copy of 
Form 700 on the county's website. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 9!014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 181 IO through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicated. 


ANALYSIS 
File No. A-11-018 
Page No. 2 
Section 81008 states that all reports and statements filed pursuant to the Act are public 
records open for public inspection and reproduction during regular business hours. The Act does 
not address the manner in which these records must be made available or how information 
appearing on a Form 700 must be displayed on an agency's website. As you indicate, Forms 700 
are routinely posted on the Commission's website with redaction of certain items. These include 
home addresses, telephone numbers and signatures. 
Accordingly, the county may post Form 700 on its website either in their entirety or with 
redaction of private infonnation without violating the Act. Because not every member of the 
public has access to the Internet, you should continue providing paper copies of these fonns, 
when requested, to comply with the requirements of Section 81008. 
VJ:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Scott Hallabrin 
General Counsel 
By: 
Valentina Joyce 
Counsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: May a local government agency post on the agency's website a copy of complete or 
redacted Form 700 filed by elected officials and designated employees?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The Act does not regulate the posting of public officials' Form 700 on a local 
agency's website. Therefore, an agency may post a copy of the entire or redacted form on the 
website.
